MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Set For Trial On May-05-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Continued To Oct-23-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Alexandra Friedman'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Alexandra Friedman's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend in part. The demurrer is sustained as to plaintiff Zakhrabova on the fourth cause of action for financial elder abuse for her to allege that she falls within the ambit of the statute. The Court overrules defendant's remaining arguments. The amended complaint does not clearly show that the parties entered into an illegal contract and defendant highlights specific intent crimes. The terms of the parties' contract is a factual issue. In addition, courts will sometimes enforce illegal contracts to the extent necessary to prevent unjust enrichment. See Dunkin v. Boskey (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 171, 195-198. The Court rejects the judicial estoppel argument. The issue is whether the amended complaint has sham allegations. The allegations in the two pleadings are not totally inconsistent and the parties agreement is a factual issue. The amended complaint alleges sufficient allegations to show a fiduciary relationship and duty to plaintiff Zakhrabova. The statute of limitations does not bar this action because plaintiffs' claims accrued when defendant took money from the account. The statute of frauds does not bar plaintiff's claims because the contract could have been performed within a year. The fraud cause of action is pleaded with sufficient particularity. Plaintiff Shapiro alleges a cause of action for elder abuse because the harm occurred when he was an elder. The Court overrules the demurrers to causes of action 5 - 10 under Dunkin. In addition, plaintiffs allege conversion/harms distinct from the bank transactions at paragraph 33 of the amended complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Jun-27-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Order To Show Cause is OFF CALENDAR AT THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. The temporary restraining order is discharged/expired. The moving party is entitled to take his application off calendar. There is no basis to keep the motion on calendar as defendant cannot seek affirmative relief through her opposition to the application. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bill Daniels, Esq. of the Law Offices of Bill Daniels, Tel. 818.907.8073, for defendant and moving party;
Michael Martinovsky, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael Martinovsky, Tel. 415.230.5360, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA FRIEDMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained as to the first, second, third, sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, tenth, eleventh, and twelfth causes of action. Plaintiffs shall have 15 days leave to amend to allege causes of action, if they can in good faith, that do not depend on allegations that plaintiffs and Rosa Shapiro have broken the law. See 1 Witkin, Summary of California Law, Contracts, 430, p. 471 (2005). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
